info.title: Joxette API
info.description: Kafka topic cassette recorder backed by DuckLake

tags.name: Catalog
tags.description: Ad-hoc SQL execution against the embedded DuckDB catalog. Intended for diagnostics and exploration.

tags.name: Streams
tags.description: CRUD for named derived stream definitions. Each definition stores source filters, optional SOL sequence processing, a transform pipeline, and an output mode under a reusable slug.

tags.name: Configuration
tags.description: Effective runtime configuration and live domain summary for operator diagnostics.

tags.name: Cassette Replay
tags.description: Replay recorded Kafka messages stored in DuckLake. Replay endpoints support three response formats via the Accept header: application/json (cursor-paginated), text/event-stream (SSE), and application/x-ndjson (streaming). All replay endpoints support optional start_at / start_delay_ms parameters for scheduled delivery.

tags.name: Transform Presets
tags.description: CRUD for named transform pipelines. Presets can be referenced by name in replay requests using the `transform_preset` query parameter, avoiding repetition of large pipeline JSON.

tags.name: Observability
tags.description: Liveness probe and observability endpoints for monitoring the Joxette recording pipeline.

tags.name: Compaction
tags.description: Manage DuckLake compaction runs that merge small Parquet files to reduce read amplification.

get.summary: Get a transform preset by name
get.description: Returns the full preset including all pipeline steps.

put.description: Replaces the description and steps of an existing preset. Returns HTTP 404 if the preset does not exist.

delete.description: Permanently deletes the named preset. Replay requests that reference this preset by name will subsequently fail.

get.summary: Get a stream definition by id
get.description: Returns the full stream definition.

put.description: Replaces the full definition of an existing stream. Returns HTTP 404 if the stream does not exist.

delete.description: Permanently deletes the named stream definition.

get.description: Returns all saved transform pipeline presets ordered by name.

value.name: staging-sanitize
value.description: Redact PII and redirect to staging

post.description: Saves a new named transform pipeline. Returns HTTP 409 if the name is already taken.

requestBody.description: Preset name, optional description, and pipeline steps

example.name: staging-sanitize
example.description: Redact PII and redirect to staging

get.description: Returns all saved stream definitions ordered by id. Pass `?entity_type=<type>` to scope to a specific entity type.

post.description: Saves a new named derived stream definition. Returns HTTP 409 if the id is already taken.

get.description: Returns all jobs, newest first.

post.description: Accepts up to 10000 entity IDs and writes the result to the configured object-storage path as Parquet or NDJSON. Poll GET /exports/{id} to track progress.

Asynchronously starts a compaction run. The run record is inserted synchronously so the response contains a valid id with status `running`. Omitting the request body (or passing `targets: null`) compacts all entity types plus the general cassette if it is enabled. Including `general` in targets enables general-cassette compaction for that run regardless of the `joxette.compaction.general.enabled` flag.

requestBody.description: Optional list of entity-type targets. Omit or set targets to null to compact all.

202.description: Compaction run accepted; body contains the initial run record

409.description: A compaction run is already in progress

post.description: Starts an immediate retention run asynchronously. Returns 409 if a run is already in progress.

post.summary: Execute an ad-hoc SQL query against the catalog
post.description:
Executes an arbitrary SQL statement against the embedded DuckDB catalog.
For SELECT-like statements the result set is returned as a list of rows.
For DML/DDL statements `affectedRows` is populated and `rows` is empty.
Results are capped at `maxRows` (default 10 000); `truncated=true` when more rows exist.
Binary columns are base64-encoded. TIMESTAMP, VARIANT, JSON, LIST, and STRUCT columns
are serialised as strings.

400.description: SQL syntax error or execution failure

post.description: Permanently deletes all recorded messages for the given Kafka topic from `lake.cassette`. Returns the number of deleted rows. This operation cannot be undone.

post.summary: Run a SOL query against a topic cassette
post.description: Loads all messages for the given topic (grouped as a single sequence ordered by timestamp), executes the SOL query, and returns the surviving events.
POST variant of `GET /cassettes/topics/{topic}` that accepts an inline transform pipeline or preset reference in the request body. Useful when the pipeline JSON is too large to URL-encode.

requestBody.description: Filter params, optional transform pipeline, and optional scheduling

400.description: Invalid transform steps or request body

post.summary: Replay general cassette back to a Kafka topic (JSON)
post.description: Reads every matching record from the general cassette for `sourceTopic` in `kafka_timestamp ASC` order and produces each one to `targetTopic`, preserving the original Kafka timestamp. Blocks until all records have been sent, then returns the final `ReplayProgress` summary. For a live progress stream use `Accept: text/event-stream`.

parameters.name: topic
parameters.description: Source Kafka topic name (cassette to replay from)

parameters.name: speed
parameters.description: Replay speed multiplier: 1.0 = real-time, 2.0 = double speed, 0.5 = half speed. Inter-message delay = (next_ts − prev_ts) / speed

post.summary: Match message sequences in a topic cassette
post.description: Scans the general cassette for the given topic in timestamp order and runs an NFA-style sequence match over the step predicates supplied in the request body. Returns aggregate statistics (totalMessages, matchRate, per-step reachRates) and up to `limit` (default 25) example MatchedSequence objects.
Triggers a DuckDB CHECKPOINT to flush the write-ahead log for the given topic's cassette, reducing WAL size and improving read performance. Returns 202 Accepted immediately.

get.description: Returns all available DuckDB EXPORT DATABASE snapshots, ordered by creation time.

post.description: Exports the full DuckLake database as a named snapshot using `EXPORT DATABASE`. If `name` is omitted from the request body a timestamp-based name is generated. Returns the created snapshot metadata with HTTP 201.

requestBody.description: Optional snapshot name. If omitted a timestamp-based name is used.

post.description: Restores the DuckLake database from a previously created named snapshot using `IMPORT DATABASE`. All current data is replaced by the snapshot contents.

Creates a named DuckDB `EXPORT DATABASE` snapshot, uploads all exported files to the configured S3-compatible object store, then removes the local snapshot directory. Requires `joxette.object-store.bucket` to be set. Returns the snapshot metadata including the S3 base URI.

requestBody.description: Optional snapshot name. If omitted a timestamp-based name is used.

500.description: Object store not configured or upload failed

post.summary: Run a SOL query against an entity cassette
post.description: Loads the full ordered event sequence for the given entity, executes the SOL (Sequence Operations Language) query, and returns the events that survive the pipeline (after MATCH / FILTER / SET / REPLACE / COMBINE). The `matched` flag is true when the last MATCH found at least one occurrence. `unexpectedNulls` lists any expressions that were null-cast during evaluation.

400.description: SOL parse error or invalid entity type

post.description: POST variant of `GET /cassettes/entities/{entityType}/{entityId}` that accepts an inline transform pipeline or preset reference in the request body.

requestBody.description: Filter params, optional transform pipeline, and optional scheduling

200.description: Paginated list of entity event records

400.description: Invalid entity type, transform steps, or request body

post.summary: Replay entity cassette back to a Kafka topic (JSON)
post.description: Reads every matching event for `entityId` from the entity cassette `lake.main.entity_{entityType}` and produces each one to `targetTopic`. Events span multiple source topics and are merge-sorted by `kafka_timestamp ASC` before producing. Blocks until all records have been sent, then returns the final `ReplayProgress` summary. For a live progress stream use `Accept: text/event-stream`.

parameters.name: speed
parameters.description: Replay speed multiplier: 1.0 = real-time, 2.0 = double speed, 0.5 = half speed. Inter-message delay = (next_ts − prev_ts) / speed

requestBody.description: Target topic and optional timestamp filters

400.description: Invalid entity type or missing targetTopic

post.description: Permanently deletes all recorded events for the given entity type from `lake.main.entity_{entityType}`. Returns the number of deleted rows. This operation cannot be undone.

post.summary: Build sunburst hierarchy for an entity type
post.description: Loads event sequences for all known entities of the given type, builds a prefix-tree hierarchy (root = start, each ring = one event step, arc angle ∝ sequence count), and returns it for D3 sunburst rendering. Up to `maxEntities` (default 500) entities are included. Up to `maxSteps` (default 8) events per sequence are considered.

post.summary: Match message sequences in an entity cassette
post.description: Scans the entity cassette for the given entity type/id in timestamp order and runs an NFA-style sequence match over the step predicates supplied in the request body. Returns aggregate statistics and up to `limit` (default 25) example sequences.

400.description: Invalid entity type or request body

post.description: Streams the event histories for a cohort of entity IDs as newline-delimited JSON. Each line is a JSON object with an `entityId` field and an `event` field containing the `EntityRecord`. When `output=state` each line contains `entityId` and `state` instead. When `output=diff` each line contains `entityId` and `event` (a `DiffRecord`). When `response_format=portrait` or `response_format=timeline` each line contains `entityId` and the aggregate result for that entity. Events are grouped by entity (all events for entity 1, then entity 2, …) in the order the IDs appear in the request. Accepts at most 100 entity IDs per request.

200.description: NDJSON stream: one JSON object per event (or aggregate) per entity

schema.description: One JSON object per line. Event lines: `{"entityId":"…","event":{…EntityRecord…}}`. State lines: `{"entityId":"…","state":{…}}`.

400.description: Invalid entity type, empty id list, or too many ids

post.summary: Rebuild known_entities registry from cassette data
post.description: Scans all entity cassette tables (`lake.main.entity_*`) on object storage and rebuilds the `known_entities` registry from scratch. Use this to recover after the local `.ducklake` catalog file was lost. Returns the number of entity rows upserted.

get.description: Returns all rows from the shared joxette_instances registry table. Staleness is detected via a 30-second heartbeat with a 90-second window. Stale rows are reaped automatically on the next Joxette startup. For real-time cluster membership, see GET /instances/topology.

Returns the live Pekko cluster membership state. Node failures are detected in ~10 s via the phi-accrual failure detector — much faster than the 30-second heartbeat used by the DB table. This view is independent of the DuckDB catalog: it survives catalog file loss.
Streams a snapshot of all running recorder metrics every 2 seconds as server-sent events. Each event contains the full cluster-state payload. The stream continues until the client disconnects.

get.summary: Unified cluster state (self + all instances + Pekko topology)
get.description: Returns a single document combining three views: the local node enriched with live recorder status and Pekko reachability, all registered instances from the DB heartbeat table, and all Pekko cluster members. Use this endpoint to see at a glance what every node is doing and whether the cluster is healthy.

Returns the liveness status of the Joxette service along with observability metrics: active recorder topics, per-topic Kafka consumer lag, catalog file size, and the amount of data buffered inline in the DuckDB catalog that has not yet been flushed to Parquet.

delete.description: Only completed or failed jobs may be deleted.

get.description: Returns the immutable deployment configuration (read from YAML at startup) and a live domain summary (topic/entity counts from config tables). Designed to help operators answer 'why isn't X being recorded' without needing access to YAML files or the server filesystem.
Returns the current compaction status including the most-recent run summary, the next scheduled cron fire time, and whether a run is currently active.
Returns the current retention status including the most-recent run summary (with per-table row counts), the next scheduled cron fire time, and whether a run is currently active.
Returns the most-recent retention enforcement runs in descending chronological order.

Always returns an empty list. Distributed compaction locking via the compaction_locks table has been superseded by the Pekko ClusterSingleton guarantee — exactly one CompactionSingletonActor runs cluster-wide at any time, making explicit DB-level locking unnecessary.

200.description: Empty list (locking is now handled by ClusterSingleton)

get.description: Returns the most-recent compaction runs, newest first. The default page size is 20.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of runs to return (default 20)

get.description: Returns the runtime compaction settings derived from application properties: cron schedule, entity and general compaction thresholds.

Returns a cursor-paginated page of messages recorded from the given Kafka topic. Supports filtering by timestamp range, partition, and Kafka offset range. Pass `nextCursor` from the previous response to advance to the next page. Use `Accept: text/event-stream` for SSE streaming or `Accept: application/x-ndjson` for NDJSON streaming. If `start_at` or `start_delay_ms` is provided, returns HTTP 202 with a scheduled replay ID instead of data.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Include only records with timestamp >= this value (ISO-8601 instant)

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Include only records with timestamp <= this value (ISO-8601 instant)

parameters.name: partition
parameters.description: Filter to a single Kafka partition number

parameters.name: offset_from
parameters.description: Include only records with Kafka offset >= this value

parameters.name: offset_to
parameters.description: Include only records with Kafka offset <= this value

parameters.name: transform
parameters.description: URL-encoded JSON array of transform pipeline steps. Mutually exclusive with transform_preset.

parameters.name: transform_preset
parameters.description: Name of a saved transform preset. Mutually exclusive with transform.

parameters.name: start_at
parameters.description: ISO-8601 absolute timestamp at which streaming begins (mutually exclusive with start_delay_ms)

parameters.name: start_delay_ms
parameters.description: Relative delay in milliseconds before streaming begins (mutually exclusive with start_at)

parameters.name: follow
parameters.description: When true, stream stays open after the historical drain and delivers live records as they are recorded. Incompatible with `to` / `offset_to`.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Sort direction: `asc` (oldest first, default) or `desc` (latest first).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return per page (default 100)

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's `nextCursor` field

400.description: Invalid topic, filter value, or malformed cursor

202.description: Replay scheduled. Data will stream at the given start time.

get.description: Returns row count and estimated size statistics for the given topic's slice of the general cassette table.

get.summary: Distinct message_type values for a topic cassette
get.description: Returns the set of distinct message_type labels recorded for this topic. Used to populate SOL MATCH autocompletion with real event type names.

200.description: Sorted list of message type strings

get.summary: JSONPath field suggestions for a topic cassette
get.description: Samples the last `limit` messages from the topic cassette and returns deduplicated JSONPath paths extracted from the message value, plus fixed envelope paths ($.key, $.topic, …). Useful for autocomplete in query builders.

Returns all replay requests that are currently pending (waiting for their start time) or actively streaming. Scheduled replays are held in memory only; they are lost on service restart. Results are ordered by scheduled start time ascending.

200.description: List of pending and streaming scheduled replays

get.description: Returns a cursor-paginated list of entity IDs registered under the given entity type, ordered by entity ID ascending.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of entities to return per page (default 100)

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's `nextCursor` field

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Sort order: id (alphabetical, default), lastActive (most recently seen first), mostMessages (highest message count first)

get.description: Returns a cursor-paginated page of deduplicated events for the given entity from its entity cassette. Supports filtering by timestamp range. Use `Accept: text/event-stream` for SSE streaming or `Accept: application/x-ndjson` for NDJSON streaming. If `start_at` or `start_delay_ms` is provided, returns HTTP 202 with a scheduled replay ID instead of data.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Include only events with timestamp >= this value (ISO-8601 instant)

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Include only events with timestamp <= this value (ISO-8601 instant)

parameters.name: transform
parameters.description: URL-encoded JSON array of transform pipeline steps. Mutually exclusive with transform_preset.

parameters.name: transform_preset
parameters.description: Name of a saved transform preset. Mutually exclusive with transform.

parameters.name: start_at
parameters.description: ISO-8601 absolute timestamp at which streaming begins (mutually exclusive with start_delay_ms)

parameters.name: start_delay_ms
parameters.description: Relative delay in milliseconds before streaming begins (mutually exclusive with start_at)

parameters.name: follow
parameters.description: When true, stream stays open after the historical drain and delivers live records as they are recorded. Incompatible with `to`.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Sort direction: `asc` (oldest first, default) or `desc` (latest first).

parameters.name: message_types
parameters.description: Restrict to events whose message_type label matches one of these values.

parameters.name: sol
parameters.description: SOL query to run over the entity's full event sequence. Incompatible with `follow`.

parameters.name: last_n
parameters.description: Return only the last N events (tail window). Mutually exclusive with from, to, and cursor. Incompatible with follow.

parameters.name: dedup
parameters.description: Deduplication policy: offset (default), value, or none.

parameters.name: state_fold
parameters.description: State fold strategy, only relevant when output=state.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of events to return per page (default 100)

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's `nextCursor` field

parameters.name: response_format
parameters.description: Response shape: `events` (default) = paginated EntityRecord array; `timeline` = events grouped into time buckets; `portrait` = compact summary.

parameters.name: timeline_bucket
parameters.description: Bucket granularity for response_format=timeline. Auto-selected when absent: minute (<1h), hour (<7d), day (≥7d).

200.description: Paginated list of entity event records

400.description: Invalid entity type or malformed cursor

202.description: Replay scheduled. Data will stream at the given start time.

delete.description: Permanently deletes all data for the given entity from `lake.entity_{entityType}` and `lake.known_entities`. Intended to satisfy GDPR right-to-erasure requests. Returns the total number of deleted rows across both tables.

get.description: Returns aggregate statistics for the given entity: total deduplicated message count, first/last message timestamps, first/last seen timestamps from the entity registry, and a per-source-topic message count breakdown.

Returns per-bucket row counts and proportional size estimates for the given entity type's cassette table (`lake.main.entity_{entityType}`). Useful for detecting bucket skew and estimating storage usage.
Returns entities whose ID contains the query string `q` (case-insensitive, substring match). Results are cursor-paginated and ordered by entity ID ascending.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Substring to match against entity IDs (case-insensitive)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return per page (default 100)

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's `nextCursor` field

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Sort order: id (alphabetical, default), lastActive (most recently seen first), mostMessages (highest message count first)

get.summary: Distinct message_type values for an entity cassette
get.description: Returns the set of distinct message_type labels recorded for this entity type. Used to populate SOL MATCH autocompletion with real event type names.

200.description: Sorted list of message type strings

get.summary: JSONPath field suggestions for an entity cassette
get.description: Samples the last `limit` messages from the entity cassette and returns deduplicated JSONPath paths extracted from the message value, plus fixed envelope paths. Useful for autocomplete in query builders.

delete.description: Cancels a scheduled replay before its start time. If the replay is in SSE or NDJSON mode the server sends a `cancelled` event/line and closes the stream. Cannot cancel a replay that is already streaming.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Scheduled replay ID returned when the replay was registered

400.description: Replay cannot be cancelled in its current state

steps.description: Replacement ordered list of transform pipeline steps

TransformPreset.description: A named transform pipeline preset that can be referenced by name in replay requests.

description.example: Redact PII fields and redirect to staging topic

steps.description: Ordered list of transform pipeline steps

fragments.description: Named fragment (phase-capture) definitions resolved per cassette; empty by default

updatedAt.description: When this preset was last updated (UTC)

message_types.description: Restrict to events with one of these message types. Null or empty = all message types.

from.description: Include only events with timestamp >= this value (ISO-8601)

to.description: Include only events with timestamp <= this value (ISO-8601)

last_n.description: Return only the last N events regardless of time window. Mutually exclusive with from/to.

entityType.description: Entity type this stream operates on

entityId.description: Entity ID to bind. Null = entity-type stream.

solOutput.description: Controls SOL output when sol is non-null

state_fold.description: State fold strategy, only relevant when output=state

StreamDefinition.description: Named derived stream definition. Stores all query, filter, transform, and output options under a reusable slug.

entityType.description: Entity type this stream operates on

entityId.description: Entity ID to bind at definition time. Null = entity-type stream (entity ID supplied at consumption time via ?entity_id=...)

sol.description: SOL sequence-level expression. Null = no sequence processing.
sol.example: MATCH order_created THEN payment WITHIN 24h THEN shipped

solOutput.description: Controls SOL output when `sol` is non-null

transform.description: Per-event transform pipeline steps applied after SOL

state_fold.description: State fold strategy, only relevant when output=state

createdAt.description: Timestamp at which this stream definition was created

updatedAt.description: Timestamp at which this stream definition was last updated

description.example: Redact PII and redirect to staging

steps.description: Ordered list of transform pipeline steps

id.description: Unique URL-safe slug for this stream, e.g. 'order-lifecycle'

entityType.description: Entity type this stream operates on

entityId.description: Entity ID to bind at definition time. Null = entity-type stream.

sol.description: SOL sequence-level expression. Null = no sequence processing.
sol.example: MATCH order_created THEN payment WITHIN 24h THEN shipped

solOutput.description: Controls SOL output when sol is non-null

transform.description: Per-event transform pipeline steps applied after SOL

state_fold.description: State fold strategy, only relevant when output=state

targets.description: Entity-type names to compact; null or absent means all types (plus general if enabled)

CompactionRun.description: Snapshot of a single compaction run as recorded in lake.compaction_history

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the run finished; null while still running

targets.description: Entity types targeted by this run; null means all entity types (plus general if enabled)

entityBucketsCompacted.description: Number of entity partition buckets compacted

filesProcessed.description: Total input files merged across all compacted tables (sum of files_processed from ducklake_merge_adjacent_files)

filesCreated.description: Total output files written across all compacted tables (sum of files_created from ducklake_merge_adjacent_files)

errorMessage.description: Error message if the run failed; null on success

sql.example: SELECT topic, mode FROM topic_configs LIMIT 10

maxRows.description: Maximum number of rows to return; defaults to 10 000

ColumnMeta.description: Metadata for a single column in the result set

name.description: Column name as reported by the JDBC driver

typeName.description: SQL type name as reported by the JDBC driver

columns.description: Column metadata in result-set order; empty for non-SELECT statements

rows.description: Rows as lists of values; empty for non-SELECT statements

affectedRows.description: Number of rows affected (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE); -1 for SELECT statements

truncated.description: True when the result set was capped at maxRows and more rows exist

isQuery.description: True for queries that return a result set (SELECT); false for DML/DDL

query.example:
match Login(login) >> * >> Buy(purchase)
if duration(Login, Buy) < 5min

typeField.description: JSON field path (dot-separated) to use as the event name when message_type is NULL. Useful for topic cassettes that have no topic_message_type_matchers configured. E.g. "type" or "eventType".

EntityRecord.description: A single deduplicated event from an entity cassette. `key`, `value`, and header values are base64url-encoded (no padding). Null fields are omitted from the JSON response.

messageType.description: Message variant label — identifies which envelope type produced this row

offset.description: Source Kafka offset within the partition

recordedAt.description: Wall-clock time when the event was recorded into the cassette

key.description: Base64url-encoded Kafka message key (no padding). Null if absent.

value.description: Base64url-encoded Kafka message value (no padding). Null if null.

headers.description: Kafka message headers. Null if the message had no headers.

Header.description: A single Kafka message header key/value pair. The value is base64url-encoded (no padding).

TopicReplayBody.description: Request body for POST-style topic cassette replay. Supports all GET replay filters plus an inline transform pipeline or preset reference.

from.description: Include only records with timestamp >= this value (ISO-8601 instant)

to.description: Include only records with timestamp <= this value (ISO-8601 instant)

partition.description: Filter to a single Kafka partition number

offset_from.description: Include only records with Kafka offset >= this value

offset_to.description: Include only records with Kafka offset <= this value

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page (default 100)

cursor.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's `nextCursor` field

transform.description: Inline transform pipeline steps. Mutually exclusive with `transformPreset`.

transformPreset.description: Name of a saved transform preset. Mutually exclusive with `transform`.

start_at.description: ISO-8601 absolute timestamp at which streaming begins (mutually exclusive with `startDelayMs`)

start_delay_ms.description: Relative delay in milliseconds before streaming begins (mutually exclusive with `startAt`)

PagedResponse.description: Cursor-paginated response wrapper. `nextCursor` is omitted when `hasMore` is false (last page). `transformApplied` is present only when a user transform pipeline was active.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass as the `cursor` query parameter to retrieve the next page. Absent on the last page.

hasMore.description: True when more pages are available after this one.

transformApplied.description: Present and true when a user-supplied transform pipeline was applied to the records on this page. Omitted when no transform was active.

solSummary.description: SOL sequence match summary. Present only when a SOL query was supplied and `sol_output` is `annotated` or `events`. Contains `matched`, `tags` (name → {from,to} spans), `sequenceLength`, and any `unexpectedNulls`.

FieldSubstitution.description:
One field-substitution rule for replay-to-topic. Exactly one of `value` (literal) or
`generate` (auto-generated) must be provided. The substitution is applied to the
message JSON body at the given JSONPath location.

path.description: JSONPath expression identifying the field(s) to replace (e.g. `$.order_id`, `$.metadata.trace_id`).

value.description: Literal replacement value. Must be null when `generate` is set.

generate.description: Auto-generation strategy. Currently only `uuid` is supported — a fresh UUID4 is generated per message so downstream systems create new entities rather than colliding with originals. Must be null when `value` is set.

targetTopic.description: Target Kafka topic to produce messages into. Optional when topicMappings covers all source topics; omit entirely for identity routing (replay back to original topics).

from.description: Replay only records with kafka_timestamp >= this value (ISO-8601 instant)

to.description: Replay only records with kafka_timestamp <= this value (ISO-8601 instant)

partition.description: Filter to a single Kafka partition (topic replay only)

offsetFrom.description: Replay only records with Kafka offset >= this value (topic replay only)

offsetTo.description: Replay only records with Kafka offset <= this value (topic replay only)

transforms.description: Optional transforms applied to each message before producing. Omit or set to null to replay messages verbatim.

topic_mappings.description:
Per-source-topic routing overrides. Keys are source topic names; values are
target topic names. Topics absent from this map fall back to `targetTopic`.
For entity replays, `targetTopic` may be omitted if every source topic has
an explicit entry here. For topic replays, at most one mapping key is relevant
(the source topic itself).

partition_strategy.description:
Controls how source partition numbers are mapped to the target topic.
DEFAULT (default) — Kafka default partitioner (key-hash or round-robin).
PRESERVE — carry the exact source partition; requires equal partition counts.
MODULO — source_partition % target_partition_count.

ReplayTransformConfig.description:
Optional transforms applied to each message before it is produced to the target topic.
Omit the entire object (or leave it null) to replay messages verbatim.

restamp.description:
Shift all Kafka timestamps so the first message timestamp = now, preserving
relative inter-message timing. Defaults to false.

fieldSubstitutions.description:
Replace field values at JSONPath locations in the message body.
Rules are applied in declaration order. Null or absent means no substitutions.

ReplayProgress.description: Progress snapshot for an in-flight or completed replay-to-topic operation

targetTopic.description: Target Kafka topic messages are being produced into

sentCount.description: Number of records successfully sent so far

errorCount.description: Number of records that failed to produce

currentTimestamp.description: Kafka timestamp of the last record that was attempted

errorMessage.description: Error detail when status=failed; null otherwise

CassetteRecord.description: A single message recorded from a Kafka topic. `key`, `value`, and header values are base64url-encoded (no padding) because they are raw bytes from the Kafka wire format. Null fields are omitted from the JSON response.

topic.description: Kafka topic the message was consumed from

recordedAt.description: Wall-clock time when the message was recorded into the cassette

key.description: Base64url-encoded Kafka message key (no padding). Null if the key was absent.

value.description: Base64url-encoded Kafka message value (no padding). Null if the value was null.

headers.description: Kafka message headers. Null if the message had no headers.

messageType.description: Message type label matched by a topic_message_type_matchers rule. Null if no matcher matched.

SnapshotInfo.description: Metadata for a DuckDB EXPORT DATABASE snapshot.

createdAt.description: Wall-clock time when the snapshot was created

sizeBytes.description: Total size of the snapshot directory in bytes

EntityReplayBody.description: Request body for POST-style entity cassette replay. Supports all GET replay filters plus an inline transform pipeline or preset reference.

from.description: Include only events with timestamp >= this value (ISO-8601 instant)

to.description: Include only events with timestamp <= this value (ISO-8601 instant)

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return per page (default 100)

cursor.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's `nextCursor` field

transform.description: Inline transform pipeline steps. Mutually exclusive with `transformPreset`.

transformPreset.description: Name of a saved transform preset. Mutually exclusive with `transform`.

start_at.description: ISO-8601 absolute timestamp at which streaming begins (mutually exclusive with `startDelayMs`)

start_delay_ms.description: Relative delay in milliseconds before streaming begins (mutually exclusive with `startAt`)

message_types.description: Restrict to events with one of the given message type labels. Maps directly to a SQL `WHERE message_type IN (...)` filter with no post-materialisation overhead. Empty or null means no restriction.

sol.description: SOL (Sequence Operations Language) query to run over the entity's full event sequence before returning results. When present the entire sequence is loaded, processed by the SOL engine, then `solOutput` controls what is returned.
sol.example:
match OrderCreated(created) >> * >> OrderPaid(paid)
if duration(created, paid) < 24h

sol_output.description: Controls what is returned when `sol` is present. `events` (default) = only the events surviving the SOL pipeline; `annotated` = all events with SOL match tags injected as headers; `summary` = only the match metadata, no events.

last_n.description: Return only the last N events regardless of time window. Mutually exclusive with `from`, `to`, and `cursor`. Result is in chronological order.

dedup.description: Deduplication policy: `offset` (default) = one copy per (topic, partition, offset); `value` = one copy per (topic, value) — for idempotent producers; `none` = no deduplication, all rows including re-deliveries.

output.description: Output mode: `events` (default) = stream of EntityRecord; `state` = fold the event stream into a single current-state JSON object; `diff` = each event annotated with changed fields and prior values.

state_fold.description: State fold strategy, only relevant when `output=state`: `merge_patch` (default) = RFC 7396 merge each event value; `last_value` = final event value wins; `last_per_topic` = last value per source topic, then merged.

response_format.description: Response shape for JSON: `events` (default) = cursor-paginated EntityRecord array; `timeline` = events grouped into time buckets; `portrait` = compact summary with counts, topic breakdown, and recent preview.

timeline_bucket.description: Time-bucket granularity for `response_format=timeline`. Auto-selected when absent: minute (<1h span), hour (<7d), day (≥7d).

BatchReplayRequest.description: Request body for batch entity replay. Supports the same filters and output modes as the single-entity replay endpoint.

from.description: Include only events with timestamp >= this value (ISO-8601 instant)

to.description: Include only events with timestamp <= this value (ISO-8601 instant)

message_types.description: Restrict to events with one of the given message type labels

last_n.description: Return only the last N events per entity

dedup.description: Deduplication policy: offset (default), value, or none

state_fold.description: State fold strategy, only relevant when output=state

response_format.description: Response shape per entity: events | timeline | portrait

running.description: Number of background tasks currently running

names.description: Names of the running tasks, sorted alphabetically

ClusterSummary.description: Cluster-level instance count from the joxette_instances registry

alive.description: Instances with a recent heartbeat (within 90 s)

stale.description: Instances with a stale heartbeat (older than 90 s)

HealthStatus.description: Liveness and observability response from GET /health

status.description: Liveness status; always 'UP' when the endpoint responds

activeRecorders.description: Sorted list of Kafka topics currently being recorded

consumerLag.description: Consumer lag per active topic; empty when no topics are active

catalogSizeBytes.description: Size of the DuckDB catalog file in bytes; -1 if unreadable

inlinedDataSizeBytes.description: Estimated bytes of data buffered inline in the DuckDB catalog (not yet flushed to Parquet); -1 on error

catalogPath.description: Filesystem path to the DuckDB catalog file

recordingEnabled.description: Whether recording (Kafka consumers) is enabled on this node

compactionEnabled.description: Whether scheduled compaction/retention is enabled on this node

instanceId.description: Unique identifier for this instance in hostname:pid format

cluster.description: Cluster-level instance count derived from joxette_instances registry

backgroundTasks.description: Summary of background tasks managed by BackgroundTaskRegistry

TopicLag.description: Consumer-lag summary for one active Kafka topic

totalLag.description: Sum of per-partition lag; -1 if Kafka is unreachable

lagByPartition.description: Per-partition lag values; empty when totalLag is -1

protocol.description: Negotiated Kafka group protocol; 'consumer' for KIP-848, 'classic' for legacy

partitions.description: Currently assigned partition IDs on this instance

DeploymentConfig.description: Deployment configuration — all fields are immutable at runtime

objectStoragePath.description: Object storage root path for Parquet files; null if not configured

s3Endpoint.description: S3-compatible endpoint override; empty string means use the AWS default credential chain

s3Region.description: AWS S3 region passed to DuckDB

inlineThresholdMb.description: Flush inlined data to Parquet once this many MB are buffered

inlineThresholdRecords.description: Flush inlined data to Parquet once this many records are buffered

compactionSchedule.description: Cron expression for the scheduled compaction run (Spring 6-field format)

retentionSchedule.description: Cron expression for the scheduled retention enforcement run (Spring 6-field format)

recordingBatchSize.description: Maximum number of messages accumulated before writing a batch

recordingBatchTimeoutMs.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait before flushing an incomplete batch

compactionEntityMinFilesPerBucket.description: Entity compaction: minimum files per bucket before compaction is triggered

compactionEntityTargetFileSizeMb.description: Entity compaction: target Parquet file size in MB

compactionEntityLookbackDays.description: Entity compaction: only compact data older than this many days

compactionGeneralEnabled.description: Whether general cassette compaction is enabled

compactionGeneralMinFilesPerPartition.description: General compaction: minimum files per partition before compaction is triggered

compactionGeneralTargetFileSizeMb.description: General compaction: target Parquet file size in MB

DomainSummary.description: Live domain configuration drawn from the config tables

topicCount.description: Number of topics registered for recording

sourceMappingCount.description: Total entity source mappings across all entity types

RuntimeConfigResponse.description: Effective runtime configuration and live domain summary

deployment.description: Immutable deployment settings read from YAML at startup; requires restart to change

domain.description: Live domain configuration summary from config tables

lastRun.description: Most-recent compaction run; null if no run has ever occurred

nextScheduledRun.description: Next scheduled cron fire time; null if the cron expression is invalid or no schedule is configured

running.description: True while a compaction is actively executing

lastRun.description: Most-recent retention run; null if no run has ever occurred

nextScheduledRun.description: Next scheduled cron fire time; null if the cron expression is invalid

running.description: True while a retention run is actively executing

CompactionConfig.description: Effective runtime compaction configuration derived from application properties

schedule.description: Spring 6-field cron expression for the scheduled compaction run

lookbackDays.description: Only compact rows older than this many days

minFilesPerBucket.description: Compact a bucket when its estimated file count exceeds this threshold

targetFileSizeMb.description: Target output Parquet file size in MB

enabled.description: Whether general cassette compaction is enabled

lookbackDays.description: Only compact rows older than this many days

minFilesPerPartition.description: Compact a partition when its estimated file count exceeds this threshold

targetFileSizeMb.description: Target output Parquet file size in MB

CassetteStats.description: Row-count and estimated size statistics for a topic's slice of the general cassette. Note: `estimatedSizeBytes` reflects the whole `lake.cassette` table, not a per-topic slice.

tableName.description: DuckDB table containing this topic's data

rowCount.description: Number of rows recorded for this topic

estimatedSizeBytes.description: DuckDB-estimated size of the whole cassette table in bytes (order-of-magnitude guide only)

EntityStats.description: Aggregate statistics for a single entity derived from its entity cassette. Message counts are based on deduplicated records only.

messageCount.description: Total number of deduplicated messages recorded for this entity

firstMessage.description: Timestamp of the earliest deduplicated message for this entity

lastMessage.description: Timestamp of the most recent deduplicated message for this entity

firstSeen.description: Timestamp when this entity was first registered in the entity registry

lastSeen.description: Timestamp when this entity was last seen in the entity registry

countByTopic.description: Deduplicated message count broken down by source Kafka topic

BucketStats.description: Row count and proportional size estimate for one entity bucket.

rowCount.description: Number of rows in this bucket

estimatedSizeBytes.description: Proportional estimated size in bytes (totalEstimatedSizeBytes × rowCount / totalRows)

EntityStorageStats.description: Per-bucket storage statistics for an entity cassette.

totalRows.description: Total row count across all buckets

totalEstimatedSizeBytes.description: DuckDB-estimated total size of the entity table in bytes (order-of-magnitude guide only)

buckets.description: Per-bucket breakdown, ordered by bucket number
